name: Managarm
description: Managarm, also known as Mánagarmr (Moon Hound), is a monstrous wolf in Norse mythology, destined to swallow the moon and stain the heavens with blood. He is the son of a giantess and a wolf, often identified as Fenrir. Managarm's existence is a harbinger of Ragnarok, the twilight of the gods, and his actions contribute to the cosmic chaos that precedes the world's destruction and subsequent rebirth. Driven by an insatiable hunger, he relentlessly pursues the moon, eventually catching and devouring it, plunging the world into darkness and signaling the beginning of the end. He is a symbol of the destructive forces that will be unleashed during Ragnarok, a terrifying embodiment of chaos and the inevitable downfall of the existing order. His siblings or associates include other monstrous wolves like Sköll, who chases the sun, further emphasizing the theme of celestial pursuit and impending doom. Managarm's role is crucial in the cyclical narrative of Norse cosmology, representing the destructive phase that allows for eventual renewal. He embodies the raw, untamed power of nature and the inescapable fate that awaits even the gods. The wolf's association with the moon connects him to themes of lunar cycles, darkness, and the hidden aspects of the world, adding layers of symbolism to his already formidable presence. His eventual defeat, while not explicitly detailed, is implied in the overall outcome of Ragnarok, where a new world emerges from the ashes of the old, suggesting the eventual triumph of order over chaos.
subtitle: The Moon Hound of Ragnarok
appearance: Managarm is depicted as a colossal wolf, larger and more terrifying than any ordinary canine. His fur is often described as dark or black, blending with the shadows of the night. His eyes glow with an eerie, malevolent light, reflecting the hunger that drives him. Sharp teeth and powerful jaws are prominent features, designed for tearing and devouring. Some accounts portray him with blood dripping from his mouth, a constant reminder of his destructive purpose.
habitat: Managarm dwells in the celestial realm, perpetually chasing the moon across the sky. He is often associated with the dark and chaotic spaces between the stars, a creature of the night and the void.
behavior: Managarm is driven by an insatiable hunger and a relentless pursuit of the moon. He is a creature of instinct and destruction, acting as a harbinger of Ragnarok. His behavior is characterized by aggression, speed, and a single-minded focus on his celestial prey.

keyMyths.title: The Devouring of the Moon
keyMyths.description: Managarm's primary myth involves his relentless chase and eventual consumption of the moon, plunging the world into darkness and signaling the start of Ragnarok. This act is a pivotal event in Norse cosmology, marking the beginning of the end of the world. The sky turns red with blood from the deed.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, including the tale of Managarm and his role in Ragnarok.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, including the tale of Managarm and his role in Ragnarok.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems, some of which allude to the events of Ragnarok and the creatures involved.
